report.summary: Built 2 new learn guides — Ethereum Layer 2 Comparison Guide 2026 (Arbitrum vs Base vs Optimism vs zkSync with full comparison table and decision framework) and Onchain Order Book CLOB DEX Guide 2026 (Hyperliquid, dYdX v4, Vertex, Drift compared with fee tables and architecture breakdowns) — with full SEO optimization, structured data, and PUBLISH_QUEUE entries.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Ethereum L2s collectively hold >$45B TVL as of March 2026, processing more daily transactions than Ethereum mainnet
Arbitrum leads at ~$16.8B TVL with 4.2M daily txns; Base leads activity at 11.5M daily txns and ~159 TPS
Optimism holds ~$8B TVL and leads developer count (3,044 devs); OP Stack powers Base, Mode, Zora and growing Superchain
ZK rollups (zkSync Era, Starknet, Scroll, Linea) collectively hold ~$3.5B TVL; zkSync Era captured ~25% of on-chain RWA market
Post-Dencun/Fusaka, avg L2 transaction fees are <$0.01; Arbitrum averages ~$0.004 per swap
Hyperliquid dominates CLOB DEX market with >50% market share, $3.35T cumulative volume, 200K orders/sec
HYPE market cap ~$9B; Grayscale filed spot HYPE ETF on March 21, 2026
Vertex Protocol offers 0% maker / 0.02% taker fees on Arbitrum; dYdX v4 runs fully decentralized on Cosmos app-chain
Drift Protocol on Solana uses hybrid CLOB + AMM + JIT liquidity model
Market fragmenting: Lighter, EdgeX, Aster each captured 15-20% share at various points in late 2025
HIP-3 enabled permissionless RWA perpetuals on Hyperliquid; WTI oil perps saw $5B volume in 72 hours during March tensions

sections.title: Content Created
items: Ethereum Layer 2 Comparison Guide 2026 (~3,500 words, 14 min read): Covers optimistic vs ZK rollups explainer, full 7-network comparison table (Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, zkSync Era, Starknet, Scroll, Linea), individual deep dives for each network, decision framework with use-case matching, FAQ section with 6 questions

Onchain Order Book CLOB DEX Guide 2026 (~3,400 words, 13 min read): Covers CLOB architecture explained, CLOB vs AMM comparison table, 5-platform fee/feature comparison table (Hyperliquid, dYdX v4, Vertex, Drift, Lighter), individual platform deep dives, emerging players coverage, institutional convergence analysis, FAQ section with 6 questions

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: Page 1 — Title: 'Ethereum Layer 2 Comparison 2026: Arbitrum vs Base vs Optimism vs zkSync' | Target keyword: ethereum layer 2 comparison | Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 questions)
Page 2 — Title: 'Onchain Order Book DEXes 2026: Hyperliquid, dYdX & the CLOB Revolution' | Target keyword: CLOB DEX onchain order book | Schemas: Article + FAQ (6 questions)
Both pages include OG + Twitter card metadata, internal links to 7-8 related pages each, comparison tables, structured FAQ sections for featured snippet targeting
Pages added to PUBLISH_QUEUE in src/lib/published-pages.ts

sections.title: Interactive Components
items: No new interactive components needed — both are learn/guide pages with static comparison tables, decision frameworks, and info cards following existing architecture

sections.title: Build Verification
items: TypeScript type-check passed (no errors in new files against project tsconfig)
Both pages follow existing learn page architecture exactly (badges, gradient H1, stats box, disclaimer, TOC, numbered sections, comparison tables, FAQ, related links)
